cards.title: The Godfather of Near-Death Studies
cards.caption: An interview with Raymond Moody, the man who coined 'near-death experience.' Niche but heartfelt. Surfaced from ~5,000 posts.

cards.title: Cited a philosophy journal for a 'biology' argument
cards.caption: The kind of dunk that only lands if you saw the original. Too much missing context to fly on its own.
cards.quote: Lol he cited a philosophy journal to support his "biological" argument

cards.title: The journey there is also funny
cards.caption: A recommendation for a dialogue on why to fund philosophy grad schools. Charming, but cryptic without the link.
cards.quote: The prescience of the conclusion is one thing; the journey there is also funny.

cards.title: Writing well is now 'anti-intellectualism'
cards.caption: A John Locke quote on learning from children, plus a sharp modern read: write clearly and people call it dangerous. Which side are you on? Curated by Threadverse.
cards.quote: These days it's sort of dangerous to play when you write like this as people will take it as anti-intellectualism.

cards.title: What are you gonna DO with a philosophy degree?
cards.caption: Every philosophy major has heard the smug question. This is the most ruthless comeback we've seen. 361 likes and climbing. What's your answer?
cards.quote: provide a veneer of intellectual respectability to a nasty hate movement targeting a vulnerable minority
cards.povText: POV: someone just asked what you'll do with your philosophy degree

cards.title: The cure for all ill is cordial speech
cards.caption: Emerson, quoted by a bot named after him, and it still hits. A small line worth carrying around. Threadverse kept this one.
cards.quote: The music that can deepest reach, And cure all ill, is cordial speech.

cards.title: Reason, revelation, whatever — don't expect me to fix it
cards.caption: The most freeing take on faith vs reason: do what you want, just don't make me reconcile it for you. Team reason or team revelation?
cards.quote: Just don't expect me to reconcile those things on your behalf.

cards.title: The one field famous for name-calling
cards.caption: Attributed to Jerry Fodor: analytic philosophy is the one discipline with zero norm against calling people names. Accurate, and hilarious. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: we are, very famously, the one field that doesn't have that norm

cards.title: Why "GORE" Is a Design Choice
cards.caption: A whole design breakdown on how game genres shape everything from pacing to gore. Indie Game Clinic goes deep. Surfaced from the noise. What genre do you defend to the death?

cards.title: What Really Happens at Launch?
cards.caption: Genuine question every gamer has wondered: is shipping a game just "bugfixing is a nightmare" or is there way more to it? Devs, sound off in the replies.
cards.quote: is it as simple as "bugfixing is a nightmare for indie devs" or is there more to it?
cards.povText: POV: you just finished a game and wonder what the devs went through

cards.title: This Week's Switch Drops
cards.caption: A weekly eShop roundup of the newest Switch releases. Useful but generic listicle energy. Threadverse pulled it, but it's low-signal.

cards.title: How Do You Actually Start?
cards.caption: The scariest question in gamedev, answered. RETRODEAD's beginner guide for anyone who's ever wanted to make a game but froze at step one. Playable demos included.
cards.povText: POV: you want to make a game but have no idea where to start

cards.title: "We Wanted You to Fall Asleep"
cards.caption: A game literally designed to put you to sleep, from devs making indie games that matter. The chaotic thumbnail alone earned the repost. Would you play a game meant to knock you out?
cards.quote: We wanted you to fall asleep!

cards.title: Agents of Groove Devlog
cards.caption: A soundtrack-and-art-update devlog for Agents of Groove on Playdate. Nice for fans, but too niche and in-the-weeds to travel.

cards.title: Big AI Chases Trillions. Indies Just Ship.
cards.caption: The sharpest take on AI in games this week: the giants chase infrastructure while small studios quietly punch above their weight. Agree or hype?
cards.quote: The biggest AI labs are chasing trillion-dollar infrastructure problems. Meanwhile, small indie studios are already using AI to punch far above their weight.

cards.title: A Sledding Game Made With Heart
cards.caption: New on Game Pass: a sledding game from The Sledding Corporation that's fun, funny, and clearly a labor of love. You can feel the passion. What's your latest cozy find?
cards.quote: it seriously looks so fun and also really funny. You can tell the dev is passionate about it and it's a heartfelt...

cards.title: The Egyptian carvings are the level
cards.caption: An indie game called Fresco where the ancient Egyptian wall carvings ARE the playable environment. Wild art direction. What upcoming indie has your attention? Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: The Egyptian carvings are actually the playable environment

cards.title: It broke and nobody knows when
cards.caption: Every dev knows this exact horror: a part of the game that always worked suddenly doesn't, and you have no idea how long it's been broken. Threadverse found the most relatable gamedev post of the day.
cards.quote: you have no idea for how long it has been broken
cards.povText: POV: you open an old feature and it's just... gone

cards.title: Use AI for what you're bad at
cards.caption: A gamedev's take on the hybrid AI pipeline: use AI for what you're bad at, keep the good stuff human. Solid but wordy. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: Use AI for what it's good at and use basic 3D for what it's bad at

cards.title: What eats your Game Boy ROM?
cards.caption: A dev breaks down exactly what devours the most ROM space on a Game Boy game. Spoiler: it's the music. Beautiful nerd content. Threadverse pulled this from the feed.
cards.quote: It's always music...

cards.title: July 2026 game releases
cards.caption: The full July 2026 video game release list. Useful, but it's a list with no payoff in the crop. Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: 45 minutes of pure jumpscares
cards.caption: A tiny horror game called Midnight Prowl: 45 minutes, packed with jumpscares that made the player literally scream, gorgeous environments. Perfect spooky snack. Found by Threadverse.
cards.quote: packed with spooky moments and jumpscares that totally made me scream

cards.title: Plant + remedy = medicine
cards.caption: A crafting game where Herbal Remedy + Plant = Medicine, with thousands of combos to discover. Fun hook but the crop is busy. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: What do YOU think Medicine combines with?

cards.title: Nobody is watching 24/7
cards.caption: Devs track metrics in a spreadsheet, then Slack, then Discord, then the App Store. Nobody watches 24/7. A clean pitch for an AI analyst that does. Threadverse found the sharpest gamedev take today.
cards.quote: Nobody is watching 24/7.
cards.povText: POV: you're refreshing your Steam reviews for the 40th time today

cards.title: AI Reviews Drop 58% For Indie Games
cards.caption: A stat every indie dev is quietly panicking about: AI-generated reviews can cut Steam reviews for indie games by up to 58%. Good news or the end of the honest review? Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: GenAI can decrease reviews for indie game on Steam by as much as 58%

cards.title: The Gap Between Shipping And Earning
cards.caption: Every indie dev knows the gap: shipping a game and earning from it are a dozen tasks apart. Reads like a product pitch though. Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: The gap between shipping a game and one that earns revenue is a dozen tasks nobody has time for.

cards.title: A Horror Game Became A Movie
cards.caption: The Convenience Store: a short indie horror game reborn as a Japanese horror film. Does the adaptation do it justice? Curated by Threadverse.
cards.povText: POV: your favorite indie horror game just became a movie

cards.title: Running The Actual Numbers
cards.caption: A dev promising to crunch real player-backstory numbers, but the post cuts off mid-sentence. No payoff to share yet. Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: A Game Jam To Build Games Blind Gamers Can Play
cards.caption: A whole game jam dedicated to making games blind gamers can actually play. Old or new, they want devs to collaborate. This is the good internet. Curated by Threadverse.

cards.title: Drop Your Indie Launch In The...
cards.caption: A weekly indie releases roundup call, but this snap is just the truncated RT with no visuals. The fuller version exists elsewhere. Surfaced by Threadverse.

cards.title: This Week's Indie Drops, All In One Grid
cards.caption: Weekly indie releases, laid out like a menu of tiny worlds. Launching something soon? Drop it in the replies. Curated by Threadverse.

cards.title: An Indie Game Outperforming AAA Gave Him Hope
cards.caption: Watching an indie game outperform recent AAA titles reminded him people care about personality over budget. What indie beat the blockbusters for you? Surfaced by Threadverse.
cards.quote: People care about personality of the product versus the budget of the game.
